{"id":"project-LICHEN-s2xu","title":"SecureDatagramChannel._process_incoming may use wrong Direction","description":"File: python/src/lichen/coap/secure.py:296-297

When decoding incoming datagrams, Direction.INCOMING is set:
```python
remote = LichenRemote(source)
msg = Message.decode(data, remote)
msg.direction = Direction.INCOMING
```

But when OSCORE protection is not detected (line 316-324), for EDHOC-in-progress peers, the raw data is dispatched:
```python
if self._edhoc_channel is not None:
    self._edhoc_channel.dispatch(data, source)
```

This bypasses the direction setting and sends raw bytes instead of the decoded message. The EDHOC channel then re-decodes the data. This is inconsistent - some code paths decode twice, others set direction inconsistently.

Fix: Ensure consistent handling - either always pass raw bytes to receivers, or always pass decoded messages with proper direction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:36.87375Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:36.873787Z"}
